core_mechanism: A coupled system of nonlinear ordinary differential equations formed by superposing two mean-field interaction models — a multi-component Lotka-Volterra interaction network (mutually coupled growth/decay with cross-terms) and an SIR-style compartmental flow (mass-action transfer between susceptible/infected/recovered states) applied to one component — whose parameter-dependent bifurcations yield emergent limit-cycle oscillations and threshold-crossing extinction of components not present in either model alone
brief_reason: The abstract couples the generalized Lotka-Volterra equations with an SIR compartmental model on a single species and reports that varying interaction parameters produces sustained population oscillations and parameter-range-dependent extinction absent from either equation set by itself, i.e. emergent bifurcation behavior of two coupled mean-field ODE systems.
